title: MongoDB Connection Pool Exhaustion on User Service
symptoms: MongoDB connection pool at 500/500 connections with all marked as in-use
User service returning 503 errors on all user-related endpoints
MongoDB Atlas showing connection count at cluster maximum
Application logs showing MongoTimeoutException on connection acquisition
Connection pool metrics showing zero available connections for 20+ minutes
root_cause: Code path in new feature deployment opened MongoDB connections in a try block but failed to close them in the catch/error handler, causing connection leaks under error conditions
resolution: Deployed hotfix to properly close connections in error handlers using try-with-resources pattern, restarted user service instances to release leaked connections, and verified pool recovery
preventive_actions: Enforce try-with-resources pattern for all database connection usage via static analysis
Add connection pool utilization monitoring with alerts at 70% capacity
Implement connection pool leak detection in integration tests
Set connection pool max lifetime to force recycling of stale connections
Add connection leak detection logging in the MongoDB driver configuration

timeline.event: MongoDB connection pool reaches maximum capacity
Alert fires for user service endpoint failures
James Wright investigates, identifies connection pool exhaustion
Recent deployment identified, connection leak found in error handler
Hotfix prepared with proper connection closing
User service instances restarted to release leaked connections
Hotfix deployed, connection pool recovering, incident resolved

embedding_summary: MongoDB connection pool exhaustion on user service due to connection leak in error handling path of new feature deployment. 500/500 connections in use with zero available. 7,200 users unable to authenticate for 25 minutes. Resolved by deploying hotfix with proper connection closing in error handlers.
